allow:
Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
Make logger level configurable per environment via LOG_LEVEL env var

the default log level should be defined in the .env files and be editable per environment

Implementation:
- Updated src/utils/logger.ts to read LOG_LEVEL from environment variable
- Created getInitialLogLevel() function that:
  - Checks process.env.LOG_LEVEL first
  - Accepts: DEBUG, INFO, WARN, or ERROR
  - Falls back to: DEBUG in development, INFO in production
- Added LOG_LEVEL documentation to .env.example
- Allows per-environment configuration without code changes
- All 355 tests passing

This follows the same pattern as rate limiting configuration - centralized 
environment variable control with sensible defaults. Developers can now set 
LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG in production if needed for debugging, or LOG_LEVEL=ERROR 
in development to suppress verbose logging.

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")
